title: AWIA Section 1433(g) certification — evidence RRA and ERP certifications are submitted to EPA through the AWIA portal on cycle and the certification audit trail is intact
exclusions: Excludes community water systems serving 3,300 or fewer persons (out of scope of AWIA s1433). Excludes systems that have submitted a paper-certification exception and where the EPA AWIA Coordinator has confirmed acceptance in writing — their evidence is the paper-acceptance letter rather than a portal-submission receipt.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.53.5` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `awia_clause="AWIA-s1433g"`. Every row pins (pwsid, system_name, population_served, tier, rra_certification_submitted_at, rra_certification_receipt_id, rra_certification_method, rra_certification_signer, erp_certification_submitted_at, erp_certification_receipt_id, erp_certification_method, erp_certification_signer, paper_exception_letter_url, certification_status). Dashboard `AWIA — EPA Certification Audit` shows the certification matrix for every CWS in scope and the audit trail of the certifying official.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evidences (a) the RRA certification was submitted to EPA on the statutory deadline applicable to the population tier, (b) the ERP certification was submitted within 6 months thereafter, (c) certifications were submitted via the EPA AWIA portal (or via accepted paper-certification exception), (d) the certifying official is recorded with name and role, (e) the EPA portal receipt id (or paper-acceptance letter URL) is captured for the inspection record, and (f) the 5-year recertification cycle is on cadence. This is the full scope of the s1433(g) certification control.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves the AWIA EPA certification submission obligation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.53.5` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `AWIA — EPA Certification Audit` dashboard, EPA AWIA portal receipt id / certification letter / paper-acceptance letter retained on a non-deletable store, and the certifying-official audit-trail row.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Set a CWS in `awia_pws_registry_lookup` with `rra_certification_submitted_at` empty and `next_rra_due_at` 60 days from now. UC-22.53.5 MUST surface within 24 hours with `certification_status="approaching_due_uncertified"` and route to the Compliance Officer with a P2 notable.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same CWS with `rra_certification_submitted_at` within the current 5-year cycle, `rra_certification_receipt_id` populated, `rra_certification_method="epa_awia_portal"`, `rra_certification_signer` populated, and `erp_certification_submitted_at` within 6 months of `rra_certification_submitted_at`. UC-22.53.5 MUST record `certification_status="current"`.

dataSources: EPA AWIA portal submission receipts (re-keyed into the GRC by the Compliance Officer at submission), ServiceNow GRC certification module, email gateway for the EPA receipt email, SharePoint Online for the signed certification letter and any paper-acceptance exception letter.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
spl:
| inputlookup awia_pws_registry_lookup
| where population_served > 3300
| eval tier = case(
    population_served >= 100000, "tier1_100k_plus",
    population_served >= 50000, "tier2_50k_99k",
    population_served > 3300, "tier3_3301_49999",
    1=1, "out_of_scope")
| eval days_to_rra_due = if(isnotnull(next_rra_due_at), round((next_rra_due_at - now()) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval days_to_erp_due = if(isnotnull(next_erp_due_at), round((next_erp_due_at - now()) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval rra_cert_age_days = if(isnotnull(rra_certification_submitted_at), round((now() - rra_certification_submitted_at) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval erp_cert_age_days = if(isnotnull(erp_certification_submitted_at), round((now() - erp_certification_submitted_at) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval erp_gap_days = if(isnotnull(rra_certification_submitted_at) AND isnotnull(erp_certification_submitted_at), round((erp_certification_submitted_at - rra_certification_submitted_at) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval rra_method_ok = case(
    rra_certification_method="epa_awia_portal" AND isnotnull(rra_certification_receipt_id), "true",
    rra_certification_method="paper_exception" AND isnotnull(paper_exception_letter_url), "true",
    1=1, "false")
| eval erp_method_ok = case(
    erp_certification_method="epa_awia_portal" AND isnotnull(erp_certification_receipt_id), "true",
    erp_certification_method="paper_exception" AND isnotnull(paper_exception_letter_url), "true",
    1=1, "false")
| eval certification_status = case(
    isnull(rra_certification_submitted_at), "rra_never_certified",
    isnull(erp_certification_submitted_at), "erp_never_certified",
    rra_method_ok="false", "rra_method_invalid",
    erp_method_ok="false", "erp_method_invalid",
    isnull(rra_certification_signer) OR isnull(erp_certification_signer), "missing_certifying_official",
    erp_gap_days > 180, "erp_late_after_rra",
    days_to_rra_due < 0, "rra_overdue_recert",
    days_to_rra_due <= 90, "rra_approaching_recert",
    days_to_erp_due < 0, "erp_overdue_recert",
    days_to_erp_due <= 90, "erp_approaching_recert",
    1=1, "current")
| table pwsid system_name population_served tier rra_certification_submitted_at rra_cert_age_days rra_certification_method rra_certification_receipt_id rra_certification_signer erp_certification_submitted_at erp_cert_age_days erp_certification_method erp_certification_receipt_id erp_certification_signer erp_gap_days paper_exception_letter_url certification_status days_to_rra_due days_to_erp_due
description: Tracks every CWS-in-scope certification submission to EPA: was the RRA certification submitted on the statutory deadline; was the ERP certification submitted within 6 months thereafter; was each submitted via the EPA AWIA portal (or accepted paper exception); is the EPA portal receipt id captured; is the certifying official named; is the 5-year recertification on cadence.
value: EPA does not retain the RRA / ERP document — only the certification. The certification IS the AWIA evidence pack to EPA. Missing it, mailing it without the exception letter, or being late are the three most common AWIA findings published in EPA enforcement actions. This UC keeps the certification ledger live, surfaces the gap before the deadline rather than after, and produces the audit trail an EPA AWIA reviewer expects on inspection day.
implementation: (1) Maintain `awia_pws_registry_lookup` so every CWS-in-scope row has `next_rra_due_at`, `next_erp_due_at`, `rra_certification_submitted_at`, `rra_certification_receipt_id`, `rra_certification_method`, `rra_certification_signer`, `erp_certification_submitted_at`, `erp_certification_receipt_id`, `erp_certification_method`, `erp_certification_signer`, `paper_exception_letter_url`. (2) Compliance Officer re-keys the EPA AWIA portal receipt into the registry immediately on submission (Day 0). (3) Schedule UC-22.53.5 daily at 06:00; route `rra_never_certified`, `erp_never_certified`, `rra_overdue_recert`, `erp_overdue_recert`, `rra_method_invalid`, `erp_method_invalid`, `missing_certifying_official`, and `erp_late_after_rra` to notable severity=critical and assign to the General Manager (escalation to the board if not remediated in 14 days). (4) Archive every row to `audit_evidence` so the certification ledger is reconstructable years later.
visualization: (Panel 1) PWSID certification matrix RAG — green if both certifications are within the current 5-year cycle with valid method and signer, amber if approaching due, red if overdue / never_certified / method_invalid / missing_certifying_official / erp_late_after_rra. (Panel 2) Tier rollup (tier1_100k_plus, tier2_50k_99k, tier3_3301_49999) showing the count of CWS in each certification status. (Panel 3) Time-to-due cohort (0-30, 31-90, 91-180, 180+ days). (Panel 4) Certifying-official table showing the signer per PWSID (used to spot a single-signer concentration risk).

references.title: Safe Drinking Water Act, 42 U.S.C. § 300i-2(g) (AWIA s1433(g) Certification)
EPA AWIA Coordinator — Paper Certification Exception
EPA AWIA Enforcement — Failure to Certify (40 CFR Part 19 penalties)

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Approved paper-certification exception** — a system has been granted a paper-certification exception by the EPA AWIA Coordinator. Mitigation: `rra_certification_method="paper_exception"` is accepted provided `paper_exception_letter_url` points to the EPA acceptance letter retained on a non-deletable store.

2. **Newly onboarded CWS within the first 30 months from initial RRA deadline** — a new system may not yet have reached its first AWIA cycle. Mitigation: KV Store exception `awia_22_53_5_first_cycle_exceptions` honoured to the first-cycle due date.

3. **Federal-system primacy edge case** — a federally owned PWS (military base, federal park) certifies through a different chain. Mitigation: maintain `awia_federal_primacy_lookup` and exclude federal-primacy PWSIDs from the search (or route to a parallel UC outside this catalogue).

4. **System decommissioning** — a CWS-in-scope is in the process of being decommissioned and consolidated into a neighbouring utility. The certification obligation transfers on consolidation. Mitigation: `decommissioning_status="in_progress"` with `consolidating_into_pwsid` populated triggers suppression and an evidence row pinning the consolidation milestone.

5. **EPA portal outage at the statutory deadline** — EPA has accepted late certifications after documented portal outages. Mitigation: capture `epa_portal_outage_acknowledgement_url` and treat as method=`paper_exception` for the affected cycle.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `awia_22_53_5_exceptions` keyed (pwsid, expires_at, reason, approver) with a maximum grant of 90 days. Exceptions for an active CWS-in-scope with no documented EPA exception letter MUST NOT be granted; the search is the audit trail and silencing it forecloses defensible inspection.
mitreAttack: N/A (regulator-submission workflow — measures AWIA portal-submission evidence and certification audit trail, not adversary technique detection)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- UC-22.53.1 (RRA currency) and UC-22.53.3 (ERP currency) deployed and emitting to `audit_evidence`. This UC compares the certification ledger against the underlying RRA / ERP completion ledger; without the prerequisites the search cannot distinguish 'completed but not certified' from 'never completed'.
- Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) authenticated to the GRC instance hosting the certification module.
- Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055) feeding `index=email` for EPA-portal receipt-email corroboration.
- Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) for notable routing, severity tagging, and audit-trail capability.
- Indexes: `index=compliance`, `index=email`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookup `awia_pws_registry_lookup` extended to include the certification fields listed in the requiredFields block. The Compliance Officer owns this lookup. The lookup is written nightly via outputlookup.
- Lookup `awia_federal_primacy_lookup` (pwsid, federal_primacy_chain) for federal-system edge cases.
- KV Store `awia_22_53_5_exceptions` for documented exceptions.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

**Day 0 — EPA submission**: when the Compliance Officer submits the RRA or ERP certification through the EPA AWIA portal, the portal returns a receipt id and an email confirmation. Within the same business day, the Compliance Officer re-keys (rra|erp)_certification_submitted_at, (rra|erp)_certification_receipt_id, (rra|erp)_certification_method=`epa_awia_portal`, and (rra|erp)_certification_signer into the GRC certification module. The GRC writes the lookup that night.

**Day 0 — EPA receipt email**: forward the EPA portal-receipt email to a monitored mailbox (e.g. awia-receipts@<utility>.gov). The M365 add-on indexes the mailbox under `index=email` so the receipt-id can be corroborated by content match if the GRC row is contested.

**Paper-exception**: if the EPA AWIA Coordinator has approved a paper-certification exception, scan the acceptance letter, upload to SharePoint Online with a retention label set to retain >5 years, and record the URL in `paper_exception_letter_url`. Set (rra|erp)_certification_method=`paper_exception`.

**5-year cycle**: when the RRA / ERP is recertified at the 5-year mark, the GRC writes a new row keyed (pwsid, cycle_n+1) and advances `next_rra_due_at` / `next_erp_due_at` by 5 years. Prior-cycle certifications remain in `audit_evidence` for the records-retention window (UC-22.53.28 closes the loop on retention).

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Schedule daily at 06:00 local time. Cron `0 6 * * *` with `dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d` and `dispatch.latest_time = now`.

```ini
[UC-22.53.5: AWIA s1433(g) EPA certification ledger audit]
search = | inputlookup awia_pws_registry_lookup ...
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.53.5,reg=AWIA,clause=AWIA-s1433g
action.notable.param.rule_title = AWIA EPA certification gap
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = critical
alert.severity = 5
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.period = 24h
alert.suppress.fields = pwsid
```

Throttle by pwsid so a single overdue CWS does not generate 365 daily notables. The summary-index marker keeps every run reconcilable to the clause for inspection.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Positive control — approaching due**: in a sandbox PWSID, set `next_rra_due_at` to 60 days from now and clear `rra_certification_submitted_at` for the current cycle. UC-22.53.5 MUST surface within 24 hours with `certification_status="rra_approaching_recert"`.

**(b) Positive control — overdue**: in a sandbox PWSID, set `next_rra_due_at` to 30 days in the past and clear `rra_certification_submitted_at` for the current cycle. UC-22.53.5 MUST surface with `certification_status="rra_overdue_recert"` and a critical notable.

**(c) Positive control — method invalid**: set `rra_certification_method="epa_awia_portal"` but leave `rra_certification_receipt_id` empty. UC-22.53.5 MUST surface with `certification_status="rra_method_invalid"`.

**(d) Positive control — erp_late_after_rra**: set `rra_certification_submitted_at` to 8 months before `erp_certification_submitted_at`. UC-22.53.5 MUST surface with `certification_status="erp_late_after_rra"` (s1433(b) gives 6 months — 8 months > 180 days is the AWIA finding).

**(e) Negative control**: full record with valid method, receipt id, signer, current cycle, ERP within 6 months. UC-22.53.5 MUST record `certification_status="current"` and clear notables.

**(f) Inapplicability control — federal**: mark a PWSID as federal-primacy and confirm UC-22.53.5 excludes it from the search and does not generate notables.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `AWIA — EPA Certification Audit` with the four panels listed above.

**Runbook for `rra_never_certified` / `erp_never_certified` / `rra_overdue_recert` / `erp_overdue_recert`** (severity P1, owner General Manager):

- Within 1 business day: Compliance Officer confirms whether the certification was actually submitted (re-key from the EPA portal receipt) or whether the submission was missed.
- If submitted but not re-keyed: re-key today, evidence the receipt email, close the notable.
- If genuinely missed: General Manager engages EPA Regional Water Protection Coordinator within 5 business days to negotiate a remedial-submission timeline. AWIA does not permit retroactive cure of a missed statutory deadline, but EPA typically accepts a remedial submission with documented timeline and a board-level corrective-action plan. Document everything to `audit_evidence` and to the GRC.

**Runbook for `rra_method_invalid` / `erp_method_invalid`** (severity P2):

- Re-key the EPA portal receipt id (most common cause: a clerical omission at submission).
- If the submission was actually by paper and the EPA acceptance letter is in hand, record the letter URL and switch the method to `paper_exception`.

**Runbook for `missing_certifying_official`** (severity P2):

- Add the certifying official (full name, role) to the GRC row. EPA expects the certification to be signed by a senior officer of the utility (typically General Manager / CEO / Mayor for municipal utilities); the GRC row must reflect the human who signed the certification on the EPA portal.

**Runbook for `erp_late_after_rra`** (severity P1):

- The 6-month window from RRA certification to ERP certification is a statutory deadline. The remedial path is the same as a missed RRA deadline: engage EPA Regional WPC, document, board-level corrective-action plan, file the late ERP certification ASAP, capture the late-acceptance evidence.

**Board reporting**: a monthly extract of UC-22.53.5 runs is forwarded to the General Manager for the board pack. AWIA exposure is a fiduciary-disclosure matter in many municipal-utility governance frameworks.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**EPA portal receipt id not visible immediately**: the EPA portal occasionally takes 24-48 hours to email the receipt. Suppression of P1 on the day of submission requires the Compliance Officer to capture a screenshot at submission and upload it as `interim_submission_evidence_url`; the row passes the search until the receipt id arrives.

**Acquisition / divestiture / consolidation**: when a CWS is acquired by or consolidated into another utility, the certification chain transfers. Mark the original PWSID `decommissioning_status="consolidated"` with `consolidating_into_pwsid` populated, and ensure the receiving PWSID's row carries the inherited certification artefacts.

**Joint operation arrangement**: where two utilities jointly operate a single PWSID (e.g. a regional treatment plant with shared distribution), only one certification per PWSID is submitted to EPA; the agreement on who certifies is the responsibility of the joint-operation agreement and must be documented in `awia_joint_operation_lookup`.

**Population reclassification**: a PWSID that grows or shrinks across a statutory tier boundary (e.g. crosses 100,000 or 50,000 or 3,300) may shift its statutory recertification chain. Re-evaluate the tier on every population census refresh and update `next_rra_due_at` / `next_erp_due_at` accordingly.

**AWIA-specific failure modes**:

- **Certifying-official succession**: the certifying official is the senior officer signing the AWIA certification, not the Compliance Officer. When the General Manager retires or is replaced, the next certification must reflect the successor. The GRC row tracks both 'signer of cycle N' and 'currently authorised signer'; the search verifies the cycle-N signer is the person who actually signed at the time.
- **Cycle N+1 acceleration after a material change**: AWIA permits voluntary early recertification after a material change. If the utility recertifies early, the search records cycle N+1 with the new submission date and advances `next_rra_due_at` from the new date plus 5 years; the prior cycle remains in `audit_evidence`.
- **EPA review request**: EPA may request a CWS produce the RRA / ERP for review (typically following an incident or a CISA advisory in the sector). The certification proves the submission; the underlying RRA / ERP retained per UC-22.53.28 records-retention is what is produced. Document the request and response in `audit_evidence` with `awia_clause=AWIA-EPA-records-retention`.
- **State primacy agency comment**: SDWA primacy agencies may comment on AWIA submissions even though AWIA certification is to EPA. Capture state-primacy comments as a separate `state_primacy_comment_url` on the GRC row; they are not part of the EPA certification but are part of the inspection-ready record.
- **Cybersecurity Action Plan overlay**: where a state has voluntarily incorporated cybersecurity into PWS sanitary surveys (per EPA's July 2024 Cybersecurity Action Plan), sanitary-survey evidence overlays the AWIA certification; ensure UC-22.53.18 (sanitary-survey readiness) is deployed.
grandmaExplanation: EPA only keeps the certification — a one-line legal promise that we did the risk check and wrote the emergency plan on time. If we miss it, mail it the wrong way, or forget to put a name on the signature line, that's the violation. We watch the certification ledger and ring the bell well before any deadline slips.
